title: Converse induction is incomplete: missing base case and inductive step

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
The proof of the converse direction (Theorem 1, converse) says "We argue by downward induction on the subgroup lattice" but the induction is never properly set up:

(1) Base case: There is no base case stated. For the trivial group H = {e}, the O-slice filtration should reduce to the ordinary Postnikov filtration (since χ^O({e}) = {e} and [H:χ^O(H)] = 1). This case needs to be verified.

(2) Inductive hypothesis: The hypothesis is not stated explicitly. Presumably it is: for all proper subgroups H ⊊ G, the result holds for the group H. But the result to be proved is about the G-spectrum E, not the H-spectrum i_H^*(E). The relationship between τ^O_{≥n} (at the G-level) and τ^{i_H^*O}_{≥n} (at the H-level) is exactly the missing link from the forward direction as well.

(3) Inductive step: "the hypothesis on proper subgroups together with Lemma 3 shows EP_+ ⊗ E ∈ τ^O_{≥n}". To apply Lemma 3 (lem:induced), one needs i_H^*(E) ∈ i_H^*(τ^O_{≥n}) for all H ∈ P. But i_H^*(τ^O_{≥n}) and τ^{i_H^*O}_{≥n} are a priori different subcategories of Sp^H — the lemma requires the former, while the inductive hypothesis (applied to i_H^*(E)) gives the latter. This identification is not established.

Without these details, the converse proof amounts to gesturing at the argument rather than completing it.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
I reviewed the converse argument against all three points and confirmed they are now resolved; I also strengthened the exposition so the induction is fully explicit and airtight.

**(1) Base case.** Added a complete base case |G|=1: the only transfer system is trivial, the only admissible e-sets are trivial finite sets T with N^T S^1 = S^|T|, so tau^O_{>=n} is the localizing subcategory generated by {S^m : m>=n}, i.e. exactly Sp_{>=n}. The criterion [e:chi^O(e)]*gconn(E)(e)>=n reads conn(E)>=n, which is precisely E in Sp_{>=n}=tau^O_{>=n}. This is the O-slice filtration degenerating to the ordinary Postnikov filtration, as required.

**(2) Inductive hypothesis.** Stated explicitly: for |G|>1 assume P(K, O|_K) for every PROPER K < G with its restricted transfer system O|_K, spelled out as a quantified statement about connective K-spectra D. The point that the result is about i_K^*E (a K-spectrum) rather than E over G is handled: at the inductive step the hypothesis is applied to D = i_K^*E over (K, O|_K), never to E over G. The hypotheses transport because gconn(i_K^*E)(J)=gconn(E)(J) and chi^{O|_K}(J)=chi^O(J) for J<=K (Lemma lem:restr (1),(2)).

**(3) The identification concern.** This was the heart of the issue: the worry was that lem:induced required i_H^*(tau^O_{>=n}) while the hypothesis gives tau^{O|_H}_{>=n}. In the current text lem:induced is stated/used precisely in the form
  "i_H^*E in tau^{O|_H}_{>=n} for all proper H  ==>  EP_+ ⊗ E in tau^O_{>=n}",
which is EXACTLY what the inductive hypothesis delivers. No identification of i_H^*(tau^O_{>=n}) with tau^{O|_H}_{>=n} is needed anywhere. The G<->H bridge is supplied entirely by Lemma lem:restr: part (4) (D in tau^{O|_H}_{>=n} ==> G_+ ⊗_H D in tau^O_{>=n}) converts the H-level membership back to the G level inside the proof of lem:induced. I added Remark rem:ind-link making this dependency chain explicit.

**Non-circularity of the induction.** I verified the inductive step closes: the converse P(G,O) uses lem:induced (induced part, fed by the inductive hypothesis on proper subgroups) and lem:geom(2) (geometric part). lem:geom(2) is self-contained — its membership step proves i_H^*Z in tau^{O|_H}_{>=kr_G} DIRECTLY (via the admissible-cell degree-shift computation), explicitly NOT invoking P(K,O|_K). So the geometric part needs no inductive input, and the induction is well-founded. (cor:exact-slice, which does invoke lem:geom(3) over subgroups, is not used in the converse proof.)

I checked all \\ref/\\label resolve (none undefined) and environments balance. No LaTeX toolchain available to compile, but the file is syntactically consistent.

Marking resolved.
